commit.message:
Add TTSim device IO tests comparing TLB and direct simulator APIs (#2253)

### Issue

Run more TTSim tests in CI. Make sure that read/write to device matches
tile_rd/wr bytes.

### Description
Adds tests that verify `write_to_device`/`read_from_device` (TLB-based
path) and `tile_wr_bytes`/`tile_rd_bytes` (direct TTSim `.so` API)
produce consistent results for the same memory operations.

### List of the changes
- Add `tests/api/test_ttsim_device_io.cpp` with 12 tests covering:
  - Write via TLB, read back via direct `tile_rd_bytes`
  - Write via direct `tile_wr_bytes`, read back via TLB
- Cross-API consistency checks (both read paths must agree after a
single write)
  - Multi-core tests with no data bleed between cores
- Repeated write/read cycles across incrementing addresses (mirrors
`DynamicTLB_RW` style)
- Register the new file in `tests/api/CMakeLists.txt`

### Testing
CI

### API Changes
There are no API changes in this PR.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: abstract storage backend with Storage trait

Extract Storage trait from ApiState to allow pluggable backends:
- Storage trait: get/set_string/list/has operations
- MemoryStorage: wraps existing @kv.Kv (default, in-memory with seed data)
- MapStorage: lightweight Map-based implementation (no bit dependency)
- ApiState::with_storage() accepts any Storage implementation

This enables future backends like R2Storage (Cloudflare R2),
FsStorage (local filesystem), or any S3-compatible store.

Unit tests added for MapStorage operations and ApiState with
custom backend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: smart scan uses single-model fast mode for CGI timeout

Root cause of persistent 404: CGI timeout (~60s on a2hosting).
The 3-model voting pipeline (Groq + 2x Gemini) often exceeds 60s,
causing Apache to kill the CGI process.

Fix: add fast_mode parameter to process_image() that uses only
Groq Scout (single model) instead of the full voting pipeline.
Smart scan endpoint now completes in ~12s instead of ~45s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add mask area validation tool with corrected calculation

Added validate-mask-area.js script to independently verify defect area
calculations from mask images. Key improvements:

- Detects grill region by identifying non-white pixels (RGB > 240)
- Calculates area using correct formula: (redPixels / grillPixels) × 5048.917
- Previous incorrect method divided by total pixels including white background
- Compares calculated values with JSON analysis files
- Shows detailed breakdown: total pixels, grill pixels, red pixels, and % Miss

Usage: npm run validate:masks

commit.message:
Move SysmemManager ownership into RtlSimulationTTDevice (#2277)

### Issue
/

### Description
Moves ownership of `SimulationSysmemManager` from `SimulationChip` into
the TTDevice layer (`RtlSimulationTTDevice` / `TTSimTTDevice`) for
simulation devices. This aligns ownership with the device that actually
uses it and removes the now-redundant member from the base class.

### List of the changes
- Add `num_host_mem_channels` parameter to `RtlSimulationTTDevice`
constructor.
- `RtlSimulationTTDevice` now owns a `SimulationSysmemManager` instance.
- `RtlSimulationChip` and `TTSimChip` pass `num_host_mem_channels` to
their TTDevice instead of to `SimulationChip`.
- Remove `sysmem_manager_` member, its include, and the
`num_host_mem_channels` constructor parameter from `SimulationChip`.
- `SimulationChip::get_sysmem_manager()` now returns `nullptr`;
subclasses override it to delegate to their TTDevice.

### Testing
CI

### API Changes
There are no API changes in this PR.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix HomeScreen cache: use module-level storage to survive tab switches

The useRef-based cache reset on component remount, causing full reload
every tab switch. Module-level cache persists across remounts, instantly
restoring data without loading screen if within 2-min TTL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: deploy to Cloudflare Workers + CI runner container

Phase 1 - Cloudflare Workers:
- worker-entry.js: ESM wrapper that defers MoonBit init to handler
  scope (avoids global random_seed restriction)
- wrangler.toml: Workers deployment config
- src/cmd/main: ESM format with fetch export for Workers
- Deployed to https://bithub.mizchi.workers.dev

Phase 2 - CI runner container:
- containers/ci-runner/Dockerfile: Node.js + git + MoonBit CLI image
- containers/ci-runner/run.sh: Step executor that reports status back
  to bithub API via Bearer token auth
- Receives steps as base64-encoded env vars (CI_STEP_0, CI_STEP_1, ...)
- Reports running/success/failure/skipped per step

Verified end-to-end: Workers webhook → Container executes steps →
status updates reflected in /actions/runs/:id

commit.message:
Fix profiler observations, prediction drift, EKF thermal mass, and coordinator resilience

- Fix _classify_mode() to treat hvac_action=None as passive drift (resist)
  when hvac_mode is not "off", so profiler records observations during
  normal thermostat operation
- Add passive_only mode to ThermalSimulator so learning-mode predictions
  reflect actual passive drift instead of assuming HVAC is running
- Fix EKF R_int_inv bounds (0.05,5.0)→(0.5,500.0) and default 1.5→50.0
  to allow physically realistic thermal mass coupling (τ ≈ 200hr vs 6700hr)
- Initialize EKF T_air and T_mass from actual thermostat reading instead
  of hardcoded 72°F
- Wrap profiler feed, accuracy tier, and model progress check in try/except
  so non-critical failures don't mark all entities unavailable
- Bump version to 0.1.5

test(e2e): expand test coverage to 94 cases

ci-webhook.spec.ts (27 → 40):

- Nonexistent workflow returns 404
- SSR without JavaScript: commits, branches, actions pages

commits-branches.spec.ts (11 → 18):

- Branch count matches seed data

commit.message:
feat: add token-based permission control for write API endpoints

- Role model: Owner (admin), Write (CI operations), Read (view only)
- Bearer token authentication on POST /api/webhook/relay and
  POST /api/actions/runs/status
- Read endpoints (GET pages) remain publicly accessible
- Seed tokens: owner (tok-owner-001), write (tok-write-001),
  read (tok-read-001)
- 6 new E2E tests for permission enforcement:
  - No token → 401
  - Invalid token → 401
  - Read-only token → 403
  - Read endpoints accessible without token

fix: completely eliminate theme flash on page load

- Blocking inline script in <head> reads localStorage, sets ALL CSS
  custom properties, then sets visibility:visible
- If no saved theme, applies paper defaults then shows page

  style, so it inherits the script-set values immediately
- Zero flash regardless of saved theme

`\$ts_N -> (elements...)` lambdas. Also fixes derive_enum test assertion for
succ/pred method count (2→4).

commit.message:
fix(tekton-ci): use comma separator in changedFilesPredicate (0.3.10)

Tekton's addChangedFiles interceptor produces a comma-separated
changed_files string, not space-separated. (^| ) never matched paths
after the first entry. Changed to (^|,) which matches at start of
string or after a comma.

Verified from live interceptor logs:
  changed_files: backend/...,vite-app/src/GoalCreationForm.tsx,...
